channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why these Jewish college students are learning Yiddish
item.description: Young Jewish-Americans have been looking for new ways to engage with Jewish culture. Some have found community by learning Yiddish, a language with roots in 10th century Europe.
item.content:encoded: Young Jewish-Americans have been looking for new ways to engage with Jewish culture. Some have found community by learning Yiddish, a language with roots in 10th century Europe.
item.dc:creator: Nirvani Williams

item.title: Smithsonian chief emphasizes 'accuracy and integrity' after White House report
item.description: The memo from the Smithsonian's secretary, Lonnie Bunch, responded to a White House report that calls the National Museum of American History driven by "a radical, activist ideology."
item.content:encoded:
The memo from the Smithsonian's secretary, Lonnie Bunch, responded to a White House report that calls the National Museum of American History driven by "a radical, activist ideology."

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: 'Music belongs to the moment': Patti Smith's guitarist Lenny Kaye is 'Goin' Local'
item.description: Kaye's collaboration with Smith began in 1971 and continues to this day. He says she taught him to trust his musical sensibilities — and to always keep evolving. Now 79, he has his first solo album.
item.content:encoded:
Kaye's collaboration with Smith began in 1971 and continues to this day. He says she taught him to trust his musical sensibilities — and to always keep evolving. Now 79, he has his first solo album.

(Image credit: Bob Gruen
)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of power ballad 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
item.description: Tyler's biggest hit is a perfect encapsulation of what made her a star in the 1980s: An epic power ballad surging with emotion, delivered in a voice that sounded like it might tear the singer apart.
item.content:encoded:
Tyler's biggest hit is a perfect encapsulation of what made her a star in the 1980s: An epic power ballad surging with emotion, delivered in a voice that sounded like it might tear the singer apart.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: Isabella Gomez Sarmiento

item.title: Young people are flocking to estate sales
item.description: Younger Americans are flocking to estate sales. Social media means a whole new set of buyers, and a new way to spend a day out with friends.
item.content:encoded: Younger Americans are flocking to estate sales. Social media means a whole new set of buyers, and a new way to spend a day out with friends.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Kurzius

item.title: Appeals court denies Trump's request to halt removal of his name from the Kennedy Center
item.description: The D.C. circuit appeals court denied all of President Trump's arguments that sought to stop the removal of his name from the Washington, D.C., arts institution.
item.content:encoded:
The D.C. circuit appeals court denied all of President Trump's arguments that sought to stop the removal of his name from the Washington, D.C., arts institution.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: A few people have played a big role in 'jamming through' Trump's arch, critics say
item.description: The design process for new memorials shouldn't be fast-tracked or dictated by a select few, experts say. But a small group of people close to President Trump played an outsized role in the arch's quick approvals.
item.content:encoded:
The design process for new memorials shouldn't be fast-tracked or dictated by a select few, experts say. But a small group of people close to President Trump played an outsized role in the arch's quick approvals.

(Image credit: Harrison Design)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: Here are the 2026 Emmy nominations
item.description: Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller presented the nominees on Wednesday morning. The Pitt led all shows with 25 nominations, followed closely by the final season of Hacks with 24.
item.content:encoded: Liza Colón-Zayas and Jeff Hiller presented the nominees on Wednesday morning. The Pitt led all shows with 25 nominations, followed closely by the final season of Hacks with 24.
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: Next gen crabbers use social media to tell their story and sell their catch
item.description: Two young crabbers are carving out careers on the Chesapeake Bay in a field dominated by men in late middle-age. One is building a brand through Tiktok. The other practices the old ways.
item.content:encoded: Two young crabbers are carving out careers on the Chesapeake Bay in a field dominated by men in late middle-age. One is building a brand through Tiktok. The other practices the old ways.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: A college student's unsung hero helped her when she got a breakup text in class
item.description: Novak's first year of college was hard. She was living in a new city, enmeshed in an abusive relationship and struggling with school. Things came to a head when she got a breakup text in class.
item.content:encoded: Novak's first year of college was hard. She was living in a new city, enmeshed in an abusive relationship and struggling with school. Things came to a head when she got a breakup text in class.
